inline_comments.message:
why not just do this once and get rid of all the tees?

exec >> $LOG_FILE 2>&1

all subsequent output (stdout AND stderr) will then be appended to the logfile

If you don't want stderr to be included, just use:

exec >> $LOG_FILE

Another alternative is to wrap the entire section in a pair of braces:
{
} >> $LOG_FILE

(include 2>&1 if you want to capture stderr too.)

As it is, it's a maintenance issue if a tee happens to be missed.
